titlestmt.titleproper: Shefter (Bert) Motion Picture and Television Music Collection
Finding Aid for the Bert Shefter Motion Picture and Television Music Collection PASC-M.0084
titlestmt.author: Processed by UCLA Library Special Collections staff. Finding aid for accession LSC-1989-01 prepared by Sage Scanlon-Perez with assistance from Emily Wittenberg in the Center for Primary Research Training (CFPRT), 2026.

p.date: Online finding aid last updated 2026 May 7 by Sage Scanlon-Perez.

address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

publicationstmt.p: Finding aid description and metadata in this record are licensed under a Creative Commons Public Domain Designation (CC0 1.0 Universal). See LSC's Metadata Rights Statement for more information.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-06-10 15:34:03 -0700.

did.unittitle: Bert Shefter Motion Picture and Television Music
did.abstract: Bert Abram Shefter (1902-1999) was born in Poltava, Russia, what is now Ukraine. After emigrating to the United States he attended the Carnegie Institute, Philadelphia's Curtis Institute of Music and the Damrosch Institute, NYC. He began his musical career as a duo-pianist with Morton Gould, known as "Shefter and Gould." Together they performed in theaters and on the radio. He went on to develop his skills as a conductor, conducting his own orchestra and was the guest conductor at Carnegie Hall 1946-1947. In the late 1950s Shefter began collaborating with Paul Sawtell, and together they produced many film scores. The collection is comprised of musical works in manuscript form including original compositions, scores for film and television productions, and some manuscripts by other composers. The largest portion of the collection consists of audiotapes and records documenting Shefter's contribution to the film and television industry.
did.physloc: Stored off-site. All requests to access special collections material must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.
did.langmaterial: A majority of the materials are in English, with some in Italian.

accessrestrict.head: Restrictions on Access
accessrestrict.p: Open for research. All requests to access special collections materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Reproduction and Use
userestrict.p: Property rights to the physical objects belong to UCLA Library Special Collections. All other rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], Bert Shefter Motion Picture and Television Music (Collection PASC-M 84). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Processed by UCLA Library Special Collections staff. Finding aid for accession LSC-1989-01 and edits to original finding aid prepared by Sage Scanlon-Perez, in the Center for Primary Research and Training, under the supervision of Emily Wittenberg, 2026.

Collections are processed to a variety of levels depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived user interest and research value, availability of staff and resources, and competing priorities. Library Special Collections provides a standard level of preservation and access for all collections and, when time and resources permit, conducts more intensive processing. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards and best practices.

We are committed to providing ethical, inclusive, and anti-racist description of the materials we steward, and to remediating existing description of our materials that contains language that may be offensive or cause harm. We invite you to submit feedback about how our collections are described, and how they could be described more accurately, by filling out the form located on our website: Report Problematic Content and Description in UCLA's library collections and archives.

bioghist.head: Biographical Note
bioghist.p: Bert Abram Shefter (1902-1999) was born in Poltava, Russia, what is now Ukraine. After emigrating to the United States he attended the Carnegie Institue, Philadelphia's Curtis Institute of Music and the Damrosch Institute, NYC. He began his musical career as a duo-pianist with Morton Gould, known as "Shefter and Gould." Together they performed in theaters and on the radio. He went on to develop his skills as a conductor; conducting his own orchestra and was the guest conductor at Carnegie Hall 1946-1947. In the late 1950s Shefter began collabrating with Paul Sawtell, and together they produced many film scores. His career included extensive contributions to film and television scores.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: The collection is comprised of musical works in manuscript form including original compositions, scores for film and television productions, and some manuscripts by other composers. The largest portion of the collection consists of audiotapes and records documenting Shefter's contribution to the film and television industry.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p:
The collection is arranged in the following series:
Series 1: Musical Projects (1954-1970)
Subseries 1.1: Motion Picture/Film Scores (1957-1970)
Subseries 1.2: Television Scores (1954)
Subseries 1.3: Unidentified Projects
Series 2: Compositions
Subseries 2.1: Compositions by Bert Shefter
Subseries 2.2: Compositions by Bert Shefter and Paul Sawtell
Subseries 2.3: Works by Other Composers
Series 3: Sound Recordings (1954-1974)
Subseries 3.1: Walter Greene Recordings (1954-1959)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents film and television scores created by Bert Shefter.

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Arranged into three sub-series by theme.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recordings of film scores, and some corresponding manuscripts.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title.

did.physdesc: manuscript in pencil; ozalid; full score

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes compositions by Bert Shefter and Paul Sawtell of the Main Title through the End Title.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a folder maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Miscellaneous songs with copyright registration forms.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Audio tape in its original box including a score copy for the conductor.

did.physdesc: ozalid master; ozalid copy; part and full score

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Original folder maintained in original order.

did.physdesc: manuscript in pencil; ozalid master; ozalid copy
manuscript in pencil and ink; ozalid copy; ozalid master; parts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes manuscripts correlated with Reel 1 M2 'The Pick Up,' Reel 6 M3 'The Creature,' Reel 4 M1 'The First Night,' Reel 2 M1 'The Romantic Week,'Reel 9 M1 'Pat in Trouble,' and Reel 8 M3 'The Disaster.'

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a folder maintained in original order.

did.physdesc: manuscript in pencil; ozalid master; ozalid copy

did.unittitle: Five Guns to Tombstone (Ozalid Master and Copy)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This was a working title of 5 Steps to Danger.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composed by Sawtell-Shefter for Samuel Goldwyn Studio.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "Boat Works," "Schluss' Boys," "Hank's Ring," "I Was a Prisoner," "Monica Betrayed."

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of a folder maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composed by Paul Sawtell and Bert Shefter A.S.C.A.P. Includes "The Reason," "Evidence Removed," "Hanks Demise," "Looking for Henry," "The Bombing Plan," "Chasing Hank Miller" and the End Title.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Original file maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composed by Paul Sawtell and Bert Shefter A.S.C.A.P. Includes "Enter Fernando," "Schluss' Boys." Note that some production titles have crossed out Harbor Lights.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Original file maintained in original order.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Includes one original file maintained in original order.

did.physdesc: ozalid master and copy; lead sheets

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "Judy's Ranch House" corresponding with Reel 5 Part 2. By Paul Sawtell and Bert Shefter A.S.C.A.P.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: From the film, "The Last Shot You Hear."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bert Shefter Productions, INC. Four track reduction to two track stereo.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copies of main title through end title along with some original handwritten versions.

arrangement.p: Contents of a folder maintained in original order.

scopecontent.p: Original label was maintained, however manuscript correlates with the film 'Motorpsycho.'

scopecontent.p: Created by Paul Sawtell, Bert Shefter, and Howard Jackson. Includes "Place to Run," "Deceptive Pursuit," "Noose."

arrangement.p: Original filed maintained in original order.

scopecontent.p: Compositions by Bert Shefter and Paul Sawtell including the main title, Bridge to Hell Entrance, To Perez's Island, Billy Escapes, Billy Reaches the Island, Billy Shoots Perez, and more through the End Title.

arrangement.p: Contents of a folder maintained in original order.

scopecontent.p: Includes Tape Legend work order, program identified as Jack Ackerman and the client is Bert Shefter Music Scores.

scopecontent.p: Includes the lead sheets for 'Dan Follows Orders' correlating with reel 7 part 1. Composed by Paul Sawtell and Bert Shefter.

odd.head: General
odd.p: This might be The Last Man on Earth (1964), as the score makes references to vampires.

did.unittitle: Unidentified Film Music (probably The Tall Texan and The Great Jesse James Raid)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents original compositions, manuscripts, and lead sheets by Bert Shefter, his long time collaborator Paul Sawtell, and other film and television composers.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: CONTAINS UNPROCESSED AUDIO MATERIALS: Audio materials are not currently available for access and will require further processing and assessment. If you have questions about this material please email AskLSC@library.ucla.edu.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents musical projects by Bert Shefter of his own and others' work.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials were processed to the item level. Boxes were previously arranged thematically and that is reflected in the current arrangement. At times an 'untitled' reel is next to a correlating reel, more information to find related reels can be found in the scope and content note of individual items.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents musical recordings by film composer Walter Greene.

did.unittitle: New Perspectives in Piano Sounds (Stereo Tape)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: David Swift pianist with orchestra. Warner Bros. Stereo Tape.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the songs "Speak Easy," "Absence Makes the Heart Grow Fonder," "Thinking of You," "Wild Flowers," "Your's Sincerely," and "Through a Thousand Dreams."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: J08P3353. Includes the songs "A Shine on Your Shoes," "I Get a Kick out of You." Total time 31 minutes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the songs "So Help Me," "When Day is Done," "My Dream is You," "Sweet Dreams Sweetheart."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Re-recording including "And Still I Love You," "There's So Much More," "I Love You So Much"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Master reel control file, client identified as Lovina Productions for production #204.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Master reel control file, client identified as Lovina Productions for production #204. Master numbers 19-61 through 19-40P.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Songs by B. Goldstein, arranged and produced by Joe Sherman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Tele-reco Music co work order F696. Modern Jazz, monaural master. Includes the following songs, Muggles, Criminal at Large, Lonely Stonely, Lavender Cloud, Blimey, The Message. 13222(8411)-13235(8432)

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Audio reel maintained in its original box accompanied with some related sheets in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Tele-reco Music co work order F699. Monaural Master dubbed from F699 Stereo. Modern Jazz, monaural master. Includes the following songs Flip Flop, Flap Jack, Get Ready, Videoville, Funsville. 13222(8411)-13235(8432)

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Audio reel maintained in its original box accompanied with some related sheets in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Tele-reco Music co work order F654-F656. Monaural dubbed from Master Stereo. Modern jazz, master A. Includes the following songs Goofy, The Trap, Scarlet Creeper, Old Gab, Follow that car, Minor Beat, Basie, Tea Party, Waiting for Action, Not Bad. 13244(8388)-13253(8399).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Audio reel maintained in its original box accompanied with some related sheets in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Sheets of paper containing information about the sound engineer, repertoire, and handwritten notes about separate parts and songs. Songs are listed in English and include Happy Day, Diamond, The Oven, Happy Hick, He Creeps. 13270(8645)-13278(8666)

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contains an audio reel within its original box along with some sheets of paper related to the reel, maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Sheets of paper containing information about the sound engineer, repertoire, and handwritten notes. Also includes a typed sheet detailing this as work order for Tele-Reco Music CO. Monaural Master dubbed from F707 Stereo. Choice takes only. Includes "He Creeps" and "What a Fuss." 13279(8675)-13291

Monaural Master dubbed from F658 Stereo including the following songs Hit em Hard, Circus March, Wagon Roll, Cowboy Engineer, More for East, Buttons, Tip Toe, Boy and Dog, Too Much Walking, and Old Gentleman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes handwritten notes and a work order from Tele-Reco Music Co. Monaural Master dubbed from F701 Stereo. Comedy (light) and modern jazz.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Notes about section one and a work order from Tele-Reco Music Co. about the master stereo tape F647 dubbed to monaural. Choice takes, master A. 13214(8667)-13221(8674).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Audio reel in its original box along with several notes maintained in original order.
